title: Yelena Belova
content:

[[Violet McGraw]] (young) |status = Alive}} {{Quote|There is something wrong with me. An emptiness. I thought it started when my [[Black Widow|sister]] [[Sacrifice of Natasha Romanoff|died]], but now it feels like something bigger. Just a... void. Or maybe I'm just bored. They send me a job, I clock in, clock out, you know? I’m in the clean-up business. This week Malaysia, next week who knows? I thought throwing myself into work was the answer. But I’m not focused and I’m not happy and I don’t have purpose and without purpose I’m just… drifting like a river. Or like an old leaf. Or like an old leaf in a river.|Yelena Belova|Thunderbolts*}} '''Yelena Belova''' (Russian: '''Елена Белова''') is the adoptive younger sister of [[Natasha Romanoff]], a [[Red Room]] operative who worked for General [[Dreykov]] as a '''Black Widow''' and a founding member and leader of the [[New Avengers]]. Following Romanoff's defection from the Red Room, Belova and other [[Black Widows]] were put through a mind-control program and turned into Dreykov's obedient subordinates. When she was freed from the Red Room's control, Belova joined forces with Romanoff, as well as their adoptive parents [[Red Guardian]] and [[Melina Vostokoff]], to bring down the Red Room once and for all. They eventually succeeded and liberated all Black Widows, with Belova directly killing Dreykov. After parting ways with Romanoff, Belova spent two years freeing many of the remaining brainwashed Black Widows before she was killed by [[Thanos]]' [[Snap]]. Upon her resurrection in the [[Blip]] in [[2023]], Belova attempted to seek out Romanoff, but was informed that she had [[Sacrifice of Natasha Romanoff|died]]. While visiting Romanoff's grave, Belova was given an assignment by [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]], who claimed that [[Clint Barton]] was responsible for Romanoff's death and that she was to eliminate him. In [[2024|December 2024]], Belova came into conflict with Barton and his protégé [[Kate Bishop]]. Belova held back against hurting Bishop, and though she came close to killing Barton, Barton managed to get through to Belova and explain that Romanoff's death was of her own choice to save Belova and the world. Coming to terms with Barton and accepting Romanoff's death, Belova abandoned her mission and departed. [[2027|Years later]], a depressed Belova was still under the employment of de Fontaine until she sent her, along with other operatives, on a mission to kill each other in an attempt to get rid of any incriminating evidence while facing impeachment. Belova learned the truth and teamed up with [[U.S. Agent]], [[Ghost]] and Red Guardian to escape getting assassinated by de Fontaine's [[O.X.E.|forces]], then they were recruited by [[Winter Soldier]] to help bring down de Fontaine and stop her plans to use [[Bob Reynolds]] as her own weapon. Belova and her team eventually saved both [[Manhattan]] and Reynolds from [[Void]], and when they tried to apprehend de Fontaine, they were publicly declared by de Fontaine as the New Avengers instead, which she went along while asserting she and the others held de Fontaine's career hostage in case she turned on them again. ==Biography== {{MD|Yelena Belova (Earth-199999)}} ===Early Life=== ====Temporary Family Life==== [[File:Yelena Belova (1995).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova undercover in [[Ohio]]]] {{Quote|You are my mother. You were my real mother. The closest thing I ever had to one. The best part of my life was fake. And [[Black Widow|none]] of [[Red Guardian|you]] told me.|Yelena Belova to [[Melina Vostokoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} Yelena Belova was born in Moscow, [[Soviet Union]] between [[1988]] and [[1989]]. During her infancy she was taken by the [[Red Room]] to be rasied as a [[Black Widows|Black Widow]]. In [[1992]], when she was three years old, she was assigned by [[Dreykov]] to play the role as the younger daughter of [[Alexei Shostakov]] and [[Melina Vostokoff]], with [[Natasha Romanoff]] playing her older sister, on a Soviet espionage assignment in [[Mount Vernon]], [[Ohio]], although due to her young age, Belova was unaware of the assignment and believed them to be her real family.{{Ref|BW}} [[File:West Chesapeake Valley Thunderbolts.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova poses with her [[West Chesapeake Valley Thunderbolts|peewee soccer team]]]] Belova joined the [[West Chesapeake Valley Thunderbolts]] soccer team, with Shostakov as their coach. Belova served the team's goal keeper, stating that wanted to be the one the team could rely on if they ever made a mistake. The team proceeded to never win a game, but Belova was always happy while playing with her team.{{Ref|TB}} Belova discovered the song "{{WPS|American Pie (song)|American Pie}}," which became her favorite song, and would have it played often. [[File:1995 Yelena Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova being forced to leave her home]] Late in the Summer of [[1995]], while playing in the backyard with Romanoff, she scraped her knee and called for her mother. Vostokoff came and helped her up and the three went inside. After dinner, Shostakov came and delivered news that the family had to leave within the hour. As they drove away, [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] attempted to arrest them with help from the local police, but Shostakov resisted them with a gun. Vostokoff, Romanoff, and Belova boarded a plane while Shostakov rode on the outside. [[File:Yelena Belova & Natasha Romanoff (1995).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova returns to the [[Red Room Academy]]]] The family flew to [[Cuba]] and with their mission over, the family was separated. As soldiers attempted to pull them apart, Belova clung to Romanoff while her older sister held the soldiers at gunpoint after taking one’s weapon. Shostakov then came and comforted them telling them that they were tough and that everything would be alright. The two were then sedated and brought to the Red Room. There, Belova was separated from Romanoff and trained to become a [[Black Widows|Black Widow]].{{Ref|BW}} ====Red Room Training==== [[File:YBelova-FinishFirst.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova finishes assembling her weapon first]] {{Quote|Прости меня.Translates from Russian to: "''Forgive me.''"|Yelena Belova to [[Anya (Red Room)|Anya]]|Thunderbolts*}} As Belova trained at the [[Red Room]], she became one of the best students. During one of their exercises, the girls were made to put together a gun, which Belova did in the faster than the other Black Widows in training. This resulted in Belova being spared from punishment as the other girls had their hands whipped. [[File:YBelova-YoungBlackWidow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova has [[Anya (Red Room)|Anya]] killed as her first mission]] For her first mission, Belova was ordered to kill [[Anya (Red Room)|Anya]], her friend also being trained in the Red Room. Belova lured Anya out to the snowy forest and hid from her. Belova soon revealed herself to Anya and apologized, as a man appeared from behind Belova and shot Anya, completing her first mission.{{Ref|TB}} Belova eventually graduated, which included being given an involuntary hysterectomy and had her ovaries and uterus removed. Now a Black Widow, Belova would complete missions for Red Room, which she had no choice in, as all of the Widows were put under a mind control by [[Dreykov]].{{Ref|BW}} ===[[Termination of the Red Room]]=== ====[[Attack on the Red Dust Cell|Freed from Control]]==== [[File:Yelena Belova EW Still.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova on a mission in [[Morocco]]]] {{Quote|What did I do?"
"Free the [[Black Widows|others]].|Yelena Belova and [[Oksana]]|Black Widow (film)}} In [[2016|June 2016]], Belova was sent to [[Morocco]] on an assignment with a team of Widows to retrieve the substance known as [[Red Dust]]: a substance that could break the mind control that kept the widows all under [[Dreykov]]'s orders and rule. The team managed to locate the target holding the cure: a former Widow named [[Oksana]]. Oksana managed to evade them but Belova caught up to her. Oksana caught Belova's hand when she attempted to stab her, only for her to drop the knife, catch it with her other hand, and stab her in the stomach, then twisting the knife — but not before Oksana managed to free Belova by spraying her with the dust. [[File:Yelena Belova Red Eyes.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is freed by the [[Red Dust]]]] Freed, Belova was horrified at what she had done, but Oksana told her that it was too late for her and begs her to free the other Widows before bleeding to death. Determined to keep her promise, Belova cut out the tracker in her leg and went dark. However, realizing this would only last so long, she came up with the idea to send the vials of Red Dust to [[Natasha Romanoff]] who was now an [[Avengers|Avenger]], figuring she would have the resources to keep the Red Dust safe.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Chase for the Red Dust|Reunited with Natasha Romanoff]]==== [[File:Yelena Belova (2016).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova confronts [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] {{Quote|You had to come to [[Budapest]], didn't you?"
"I came here because I thought you wouldn't.|[[Natasha Romanoff]] and Yelena Belova|Black Widow (film)}} Laying low, Belova hid out in a [[S.H.I.E.L.D. Safe House|safe house]] in [[Budapest]], [[Hungary]] provided by [[Rick Mason]], only to be reunited with [[Natasha Romanoff]]. The two confronted each other at gunpoint, which led to a brutal confrontation, as the two of them fought throughout the apartment. The brawl concluded with the sisters choking each other with a curtain, so they call for a truce. [[File:YelenaAndNatasha - Black Widow-Still.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova tells [[Natasha Romanoff]] about the [[Red Room]]]] Romanoff enquired as to why Belova sent her the [[Red Dust]], so she explained what it was and how she thought Romanoff would have gotten her fellow [[Avengers]] to use them to bring down the [[Red Room]]. This shocked Romanoff, as she believed the Red Room, and in addition [[Dreykov]], were no more. Belova asked Romanoff to recount her [[Budapest Operation|mission]] in Budapest to kill Dreykov, so she did, but when [[Antonia Dreykov]] was brought up, Romanoff went silent. [[File:BlackWidow - Still - NatashaAndYelena.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova escapes with [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] At that moment, [[Black Widows]] surrounded the apartment, and Belova and Romanoff were forced to escape by way of the rooftops to the other side of the complex, where Belova's motorbike was. They pushed a pipe over the side of the building to escape. A [[Ingrid (Widow)|Widow]] also jumped on, but lost her grip and fell into the alley below. Belova ended up crashing through a window while Romanoff landed in the alley next to the Widow, who committed suicide in front of Romanoff against her will. After recovering, Belova reconvened with Romanoff. Romanoff asked her how many Widows were out there, and Belova replied enough and went to the motorcycle. Romanoff drove it while Belova jumped on behind her and the two tried to escape from the Widows. [[File:Black Widow - You Got a Plan 11.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova eliminates a [[Black Widows|Widow]] in chase]] However, they soon crashed and were thrown off the motorcycle. In order to keep escaping, Belova hijacked a car, forcing a Hungarian man out by gunpoint, to Romanoff's displeasure, and jumped inside. As Romanoff drove them through the city, Belova asked what plan Romanoff had to evade the Widows, but Romanoff replies that her plan was just to drive them away. In order to get rid of a Widow pursuing them, Belova opened her car door, causing it to hit a pole and fly into the Widow, knocking her out. [[File:Yelena face with light.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova learns of the [[Budapest Operation]]]] They were then pursued by [[Taskmaster]], who shot an explosive arrow at the car, causing it to topple over and land in [[Nyugati Station]]. Belova and Romanoff got out of the wreckage, with Belova injuring her arm, but Romanoff told her not to bandage it so that they could create a fake blood trail to lure Taskmaster away. The two sisters hid in a vent, and waited for Taskmaster to lose track of them, with Romanoff explaining what happened on her mission in Budapest.{{Ref|BW}} ====Sisterly Bonding==== [[File:Yelena and Natasha.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova discusses the [[Red Room]]]] {{Quote|Well, then you were fooling yourself because pain and suffering is every day and we are both still a trained killer. Except I'm not the one that's on the cover of a magazine. I'm not the killer that little girls call their hero.|Yelena Belova to [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} Once they were in the clear, Belova and Romanoff went to a gas station to get medical supplies. While they were there, Romanoff asked if Belova knew where the [[Red Room Academy|Red Room]] was, but she didn't know, as [[Dreykov]] moved locations constantly. [[File:Black Widow Pose.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova jokes about the [[Black Widow]] pose]] Belova questioned Romanoff on why she never attempted to contact or find her, which Romanoff replied was because she thought Belova was living a normal life and didn't want to see her. But Belova didn't believe this, as she saw it as Romanoff just not wanting to her to tag along as she saved the world with the Avengers. Belova then asked about Romanoff's posing during fights, which she defended was done during her attempt to do some good, but Belova called them both out for being still trained killers, only that Romanoff was viewed as a hero. [[File:Black Widow - Celebrate the Movies 06.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova teams up with [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] As they shared drinks, Romanoff stitched up Belova's arm as she explained more about the [[Red Dust]] and her time with the Red Room, expressing her infuriation at being made to kill with no independence and Dreykov still being able to abduct and brainwash young girls like them with no consequences. They then talked about their real parents, with Belova expressing her fantasy backstory she made for herself and Romanoff, and that she wanted to get a [[Dogs|dog]]. With nowhere to go, Belova decided to help Romanoff kill Dreykov and end the Red Room together. [[File:Yelena Belova & Rick Mason.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is assisted by [[Rick Mason]]]] Belova also got a [[Yelena Belova's Vest|vest]], which was the first thing she's ever bought, as she wanted to do things now that she was free. Belova told Romanoff this as they drove, with Romanoff, knowing that Belova was just vying for her approval, initially teased her about the vest, but later admitted that it was cool. Belova and Romanoff arrived in [[Norway]], where they were met by [[Rick Mason]] who supplied them with a helicopter, instead of a jet, so Belova teased him. Belova boarded the aircraft, and she and Romanoff changed into the white suits provided by Mason as they went to rescue their adopted [[Red Guardian|father]].{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Escape from the Seventh Circle Prison|Prison Breakout]]==== [[File:Black Widow - Celebrate the Movies 08.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova fires a missile at [[Seventh Circle Prison]]]] {{Quote|Whoa... This would be a cool way to die.|Yelena Belova|Black Widow (film)}} Flying to the [[Seventh Circle Prison]] in [[Russia]] co-piloted the helicopter, as [[Natasha Romanoff]] instructed [[Alexei Shostakov]] in escaping from the prison. Belova stayed in the helicopter as Romanoff went down to assist Shostakov, but soon came under fire, so she used a missile launcher, which caused an avalanche. Now with time running out, Belova quickly picked up Romanoff and Shostakov before they were killed in the avalanche, successfully escaping from the prison. [[File:Got This Black Widow 03.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova with [[Natasha Romanoff]] and [[Alexei Shostakov]]]] With Shostakov onboard, Belova punched him in the face, which made him ask if she was on her period, so Belova corrected him by telling him how she no longer had any reproductive organs due to the [[Red Room]] giving involuntary hysterectomies. When they asked Shostakov about the Red Room, he did not where it was, so he suggested they ask [[Melina Vostokoff]], who could be found outside of [[Russia|St. Petersburg]]. Belova noted that they were low on fuel, but Shostakov told her they could make it.{{Ref|BW}} ====Family Reunion==== [[File:Alexei smells really bad.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is hugged by [[Alexei Shostakov]]]] {{Quote|That wasn't real. Who cares?"
"Don't say that. Please don't say that. It was real. It was real to me.|[[Natasha Romanoff]] and Yelena Belova|Black Widow (film)}} Upon landing somewhat roughly due to the helicopter having run out of fuel, Belova and [[Natasha Romanoff]] were hugged by [[Alexei Shostakov]] who voiced how proud he was of his daughters for their respective achievements. A disgusted and angered Romanoff quickly broke from his grasp as Shostakov held Belova, patted her, and gave her a kiss on the forehead. However, Belova remarked on how bad he smelled, due to having been in prison and broke the embrace as well. [[File:Yelena and Melina.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova speaks with [[Melina Vostokoff]]]] Eventually the three arrived at [[Melina Vostokoff]]’s farm, where the reunited “family” sat at a table for dinner. Belova sat down with Romanoff, Vostokoff, and Shostakov, who had changed into his [[Red Guardian]] suit. They then discussed Vostokoff’s involvement in the [[Red Room]] as she revealed that their successful mission in [[Ohio]] twenty-one years ago was to achieve the ability to effectively mind control a person and take their free will from them, with Vostokoff demonstrating this on one of her pigs, Alexei, by commanding it to stop breathing before reviving it. [[File:BW FT 76.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova gets upset with her family]] As the conversation became more heated, Romanoff bluntly denied the significance of their family and claimed that it wasn’t real. A heartbroken Belova, however, voiced how she believed their family was real even though she knew the history behind it, and vented her anger at Vostokoff and Shostakov for their part in her becoming a killer with no free will in the Red Room and Romanoff for doing nothing to help her once she managed to escape and stormed off. [[File:Yelena Belova and Red Guardian.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova bonds with [[Alexei Shostakov]]]] While in a bedroom, Shostakov joined her and despite insisting she did not want to talk he stayed anyway in silence with her for a little while. However he ended up telling her a story of a fishing incident with his father that had gone wrong and ended with his father having to urinate in his hands to prevent frostbite from taking over, much to Belova’s annoyance and disgust as she stopped him and questioned the relevance of the story. Shostakov attempted to explain to her that fathers will try to do their best for their children, but Belova scoffed at this, reminding him that since they freed him, he had done nothing but essentially complain about how bored and annoyed he was with the assignment of having to act as her father despite the fact that to her he had meant everything and she loved him like a father before telling him to get out. [[File:Yelena Belova smiles.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova smiles at the "American Pie" song]] Shostakov seemed to comply before stopping and began singing “American Pie.” This was Belova's favorite song as a child and she smiled upon hearing it, delighted to bond with her father. Together the two sang the song. However this moment of bonding was interrupted when the [[Black Widows]] located the farm and subdued Shostakov with numerous tranquilizer darts. Belova ran out of the room, only to be shocked unconscious shortly after by "Vostokoff," Romanoff in disguise, and taken hostage upon the Widows' helicopter.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Destruction of the Red Room]]==== [[File:Yelena Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova back in the [[Red Room]]]] {{Quote|This was fun!|Yelena Belova to [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} Arriving at the [[Red Room Academy]], Belova, still unconscious, was taken to be operated on so that [[Dreykov]] would know how the [[Red Dust]] altered the chemicals in her brain. Belova was contacted by [[Melina Vostokoff]], who had put a communication device in her ear, informing her of a blade hidden in her suit. Belova used the blade to cut herself free and kill the surgeons, and then went to retrieve the vials to free the [[Black Widows]]. [[File:The New Black Widow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova strikes a pose]] Making her way through the ceiling vents to cold storage, Belova dropped down, making a fighting pose like [[Natasha Romanoff]], much to her disgust. Belova swiftly eliminated a guard, located the vials of the Red Dust, and began making her way to the Widows, all while being updated by Vostokoff on her progress. After learning that the Red Room was falling from the sky, Belova entered the training room, only to find it empty. [[File:Yelena frees the Widows.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova frees the [[Black Widows]]]] Belova eventually found all the Widows, who were in Dreykov's office brutally attacking Romanoff. Having wrapped the vials around a grenade, Belova threw it over the Widows, causing the Red Dust to burst, ultimately freeing them from Dreykov's control. Belova helped her sister up and removed a knife from her back, and began to evacuate with the other Widows, as Romanoff stayed to collect the data on the other Widows. [[File:Yelena BW Total Film Still.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova runs towards [[Dreykov]]]] Seeing Dreykov escaping in his helicopter, Belova ran after them on the runway while the Red Room exploded behind her. She climbed on top of the helicopter next to a propeller and started to put together her baton, intending to stick it in the propeller and break the helicopter, preventing Dreykov's escape in a self-sacrificing move. [[File:Black Widow Yelena Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova breaks the helicopter's propeller]] As Dreykov ordered his men to shoot at her, Romanoff ran out of the Red Room and saw her on the helicopter. She begged Belova not to do it, but she instead yelled out about how their adventure was fun before plunging her baton into the propeller, destroying the helicopter and throwing her over the edge of the ship. [[File:Natasha Romanoff and Yelena Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is given a parachute by [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] As Belova was in free fall, plummeting towards the ground, Romanoff, also jumping off, caught up to her, having acquired a parachute, and attached it to her as they fell through the sky. Romanoff held onto Belova as they descended, but she soon had to let go when [[Taskmaster]] came after them, allowing Belova to land safely, while they fought in the sky.{{Ref|BW}} ====Family Split Apart==== [[File:BW FT 50.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova and [[Natasha Romanoff]] reconcile]] {{Quote|Besides, if it can work out with the four of us, you know, there may be some hope for the [[Avengers]]. Little bit."
"Okay, well if you're leaving, then I guess you should take [[Yelena Belova's Vest|this]]. I know how much you like it.|[[Natasha Romanoff]] and Yelena Belova|Black Widow (film)}} Belova was found on the ground by [[Natasha Romanoff]], who apologized for not coming back for her when she left the [[Red Room]], revealing that their family was real for her, too. This deeply touched Belova, and the two sisters emotionally embraced each other, as they were then rejoined by [[Melina Vostokoff]] and [[Alexei Shostakov]]. [[File:Natasha and Yelena - BlackWidow.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Belova says goodbye to [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] When [[Thaddeus Ross]] and his soldiers were approaching, Romanoff told her family to escape and that she would stay, but they all objected the leaving her behind. However, Romanoff was determined, as after repairing her family, she decided to go fix her [[Avengers]] family, so Belova decided to give Romanoff her [[Yelena Belova's Vest|vest]]. Romanoff gave her the last vial of [[Red Dust]] so she could help the other [[Black Widows]]. The freed Widows landed, and Belova, Vostokoff and Shostakov departed with them, saying goodbye to Romanoff.{{Ref|BW}} ===Freeing the Black Widows=== [[File:Sonya and Yelena.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova and [[Sonya]] enter [[Ana]]'s [[Ana's House|house]]]] {{Quote|It's hard work. It's hard finding [[Black Widows|them]]. It's... It's hard watching them wake. But it's so worth it.|Yelena Belova to [[Ana]]|Ronin (episode)}} Belova, alongside [[Red Guardian]] and [[Melina Vostokoff]], mounted an effort to search the world for remaining [[Black Widows]] to free them from [[Dreykov]]'s control, and deprogram their chemical-altered mind controls.{{Ref|BW}} In [[2018|May 2018]], Belova and [[Sonya]] tracked down [[Ana]] and infiltrated her [[Ana's House|house]] where they approached the ex-Black Widow herself. [[File:Yelena Belova gun.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova confronts [[Ana]]]] Ana engaged in a fight with them, with Belova pointing a gun at her face, but Belova did not shoot, so Ana was able to disarm her. Eventually, Belova managed to gain an upper hand and while Sonya pinned Ana to the floor, Belova sprayed her with the [[Red Dust]]. However, to Belova's surprise, Ana was revealed to not being brainwashed by Dreykov, as she managed to purchase the house by working as an assassin for a living. [[File:Yelena (2018).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova speaks with [[Ana]]]] After making a truce, Belova and Ana talked about her mission to save ex-Black Widows and Belova shared her results, saying that although her mission was difficult, it was worth it. Ana then asked Belova about [[Natasha Romanoff]], to which she replied that her sister was doing well. Sonya then jokingly said that after they are done, Belova could reunite with Romanoff and live in [[New York City]] together, after which Belova left them to take a bathroom break.{{Ref|HE105}} ====Snap and Blip==== [[File:Yelena Belova Blip.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova being [[Blip|restored to life]]]] {{Quote|Ana, I was in there for five seconds, I come out and I've lost five years of my life. Please."
"I'm sorry, Yelena. I was just trying to help you."
"I know, I... Thank you. Yeah. I need to find [[Natasha Romanoff|Natasha]].|Yelena Belova and [[Ana]]|Ronin (episode)}} In the bathroom, Belova stared at the mirror, slowly collecting herself before turning on the faucet. During this time, Belova found herself [[Snap|disintegrating into dust]] and could barely react before she found herself instantly [[Blip|rematerializing]]. [[File:Yelena Belova (2023).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova observes her location]] Belova became increasingly confused upon noticing the walls beginning to change around her and stepped out of the bathroom to see the house completely renovated along with a man and a little girl in the living room. Belova then raised her weapon, demanding to know what was going on and asked the man who he was, but [[Ana]] came in and defused the situation. She told her husband and daughter that Belova was an old friend. [[File:Yelena (2023).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova learns what happened from [[Ana]]]] Ana informed Belova that it had been five years since they last met, and that half of the world's population had also been erased from existence as well, during which she married and adopted a daughter. Ana suggested that Belova perform contract work and offered her to stay at her house for a while, but Belova, realizing that the year was now [[2023]] and that she missed out five years of her life, declined and told her that she needed to contact her [[Black Widow|sister]] and let her know that she was okay.{{Ref|HE105}} ===Hunting Clint Barton=== ====Mourning Natasha Romanoff==== [[File:Yelena Belova Post-Blip.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova visits [[Natasha Romanoff]]'s grave]] {{Quote|You're not supposed to be bothering me on my holiday time, Valentina.|Yelena Belova to [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]]|Black Widow (film)}} Belova eventually discovered that [[Natasha Romanoff]] had died and erected a gravestone for her in [[Ohio]], as well as acquiring a pet [[Dogs|dog]] whom she named [[Fanny]]. Abandoning her previous mission of freeing other [[Black Widows]], she instead met [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] and worked for her on target operations. In [[2024|December 2024]], Belova paid a visit to Romanoff's grave, straightening out the stuffed animals, cards, and flowers around the grave while mourning the loss of her sister. She whistled the beginning of the tune that Romanoff would usually return, but no one responded, leading Belova to become emotional. [[File:Valentina Allegra de Fontaine & Yelena Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova speaks with [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]]]] While Belova was mourning, she was interrupted by De Fontaine blowing her nose. Belova promptly told de Fontaine that she was on holiday leave and wanted a raise. De Fontaine said she could earn it with her next assignment and showed her a picture of [[Clint Barton]], claiming that he was responsible for Romanoff's death and indicating him as the next target.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Infiltration into Maya Lopez's Apartment|Attacking Clint Barton]]==== [[File:Yelena v Hawkeye.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova arrives at the roof to kill [[Clint Barton]]]] {{Quote|I did not try to kill you. A, I put you on a wire to remove an obstacle. De Fontaine ordered Sentry to apprehend the Thunderbolts, manipulating him to think the team were against him, so Belova begged him not to listen to de Fontaine, stating that she knew who he was. However, Sentry responded that Belova did not. [[File:Thunderbolts Super Bowl Trailer (17).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova witnesses [[Sentry]] defeat the [[Thunderbolts|team]]]] [[Red Guardian]] attacked Sentry, commencing a fight between them, so Belova tried to get the Thunderbolts to stand down, but failed. Belova witnessed as her team were quickly bested, so she snuck up being Sentry and jumped on him, zapping Sentry with her [[Black Widow's Bite|Widow's Bite]] and telling him to stop. Sentry did not listen, and instead slammed Belova into the ceiling before throwing her aside. Sentry then defeated the rest of the team, forcing them all to retreat into the elevator.{{Ref|TB}} ====Emotional Breakdown==== [[File:Yelena insults the Thunderbolts.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova snaps and insults the [[Thunderbolts]]]] {{Quote|Daddy, I'm so alone. I don't have anything anymore. All I do is sit and look at my phone and think of all the terrible things that I've done, and then I go to work, and then I drink and then I come home to no one, and I sit and think about all the terrible things I've done again and again.|Yelena Belova to [[Red Guardian]]|Thunderbolts*}} The [[Thunderbolts]] walked out of the [[Watchtower]], and as they argued about their next course of actions, Belova snapped and rejected them being a team. Belova proceeded to insult the entire time, pointing out how they were all bad people and calling them losers before walking away. [[File:Yelena crying.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova breaks down in front of her [[Alexei Shostakov|father]]]] [[Alexei Shostakov]] chased after Belova, who wanted to be left alone, but he continued to follow her. Belova stopped and confronted her father for disappearing on her after [[Natasha Romanoff]] died. Shostakov apologized, claiming he thought Belova did not want him. Belova tearfully opened up about how she was unhappy with her life due to the bad things she had done. Shostakov told his daughter they had all done bad things, but Belova was overwhelmed by hers, recounting how she had to kill [[Anya (Red Room)|Anya]]. [[File:Thunderbolts Super Bowl Trailer (25).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is comforted by [[Alexei Shostakov]]]] Shostakov comforted Belova by telling her that before being taken by the [[Red Room]] she was a happy child, although she did not remember any of that. Shostakov then brought up why Belova chose to be the goal keeper on her [[West Chesapeake Valley Thunderbolts|soccer team]], which was because she wanted to be the one people could rely on if they made a mistake. Shostakov finished by telling Belova that he still saw the good in her, which was why they needed each other. Belova was touched by her father's words and admitted that his speech worked.{{Ref|TB}} ====[[Attack on Manhattan|Entering the Void]]==== [[File:Thunderbolts Super Bowl Trailer (19).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova witnesses [[Bob Reynolds]] as the [[Void]]]] {{Quote|It's what you said. We're all alone. All of us.|[[Void]] to Yelena Belova|Thunderbolts*}} Belova and [[Red Guardian]] discovered [[Bob Reynolds]] in the sky, although now he had been taken over by the Void, which worried Belova. The Void began to attack, causing debris to fall, endangering the civilians, so Belova followed after Red Guardian to protect them. Belova spotted a truck heading for an old woman, so she had Red Guardian boost her over to rescue the woman. The rest of the [[Thunderbolts]] arrived to save the civilians. [[File:YBelova-CrossingTheVoid.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova makes the choice to enter the [[Void]]]] When [[U.S. Agent]] was struggling to hold up a giant piece of debris by himself, the rest of the team rushed to help him, successfully tipping it over. The Thunderbolts were all applauded by the people they had saved, which took them by surprise. The Thunderbolts witnessed the Void vanish several civilians, causing everyone to flee, except Belova, who approached the Void, who repeated Belova's words about everyone being alone. Knowing that Reynolds needed to be saved, Belova decided to step into the Void. [[File:Belova's Flashback.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is trapped in her own shame room]] Belova found herself back in the memory of when she had to kill [[Anya (Red Room)|Anya]]. Belova ignored the memory playing out and went to find Reynolds, but ran into a wall, trapping her. The memory replayed, so Belova went to stop Anya from being killed, only to be attacked by Anya and restrained by a tree, forcing her to watch her painful memory. Belova managed to break free and ran away, crashing through the wall and entering another memory. [[File:YBelova-CoveringHerPast.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova attempts to protect her younger self]] Belova fell onto a table inside of the [[Red Room Academy]] during a memory when she and a group of other girls were forced to assemble a gun against one another. Belova protected her younger self from witnessing the other girls being whipped due to her being the fastest in the group. Belova spotted Reynolds in the mirror, but when she looked again she discovered that he was gone. Belova figured Reynolds was in the next room, so she shot at the mirror and crashed through it. [[File:Yelena vs. Yelena.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is attacked by a version of herself]] Belova instead dropped into her bathroom, where she found her past self passed out from drinking. Belova went to inspect her past self but was attacked by her. Belova was slammed against the wall and forced to drink from a bottle of alcohol until she fell down to the ground. The memory reset, so Belova called out to Reynolds, telling him to stop what he was doing. Reynolds appeared to Belova through the mirror, so she told him that she wanted to talk about what they discusses in the [[O.X.E. Vault]].{{Ref|TB}} ====[[Liberation of Bob Reynolds|Saving Bob Reynolds]]==== [[File:Thunderbolts D23 Brazil Special Look (25).png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova learns about the [[Void]] from [[Bob Reynolds|Reynolds]]]] {{Quote|We have to let it out. We have to spend time together. And even if it doesn't make the emptiness go away I promise you it will feel lighter."
"How do you know?"
"'Cause it already has for me.|Yelena Belova and [[Bob Reynolds]]|Thunderbolts*}} Belova suddenly appeared in the same room as Reynolds and asked him to stop his [[Attack on Manhattan]], but he replied that he was not responsible, it was the Void. Belova listened as Reynolds explained how his mental state shifted, making him feel as though nothing mattered and that there was no point fighting the Void. [[File:Yelena conforting Bob.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova provides [[Bob Reynolds]] with comfort]] Overhearing commotion, Belova peaked into an adjoined room and witnessed Reynolds' memory of his father abusing his mother, who blamed Reynolds. Belova expressed sympathy towards Reynolds and sat down next to him to comfort him. Belova told Reynolds that her advice to push down his emotions was wrong, and instead they needed to let it out and spend time together. Reynolds questioned how Belova was sure, so she answered that it already worked for her being with her new friends. [[File:Belova & Reynolds in the attic's chaos.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova and [[Bob Reynolds]] are attacked]] Belova convinced Reynolds to try to escape from the Void together, but they were then attacked by objects in the room. Unable to fight back against the chaos, Belova and Reynolds were soon strangled by a curtain. They were saved when [[Ava Starr]] appeared and cut them free, followed by [[Bucky Barnes]], [[John Walker]], and [[Alexei Shostakov]], who all entered the Void to save them. Reynolds explained that the Void was just endless rooms, so Belova decided to head for the worst one as their way of escape. [[File:Thunderbolts Team.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|The [[Thunderbolts|team]] and [[Bob Reynolds]] find the [[Void]]]] The Thunderbolts made their way through Reynolds' memories, briefly being attacked by a past version of him dressed as a chicken mascot. They soon found themselves in the [[O.X.E. Kuala Lumpur Research Facility]], where Reynolds was experiment on, and they were confronted by the Void. Belova told it they were leaving, but the Void refused and pinned Belova and Shostakov against the wall. The rest of the team were restrained except for Reynolds, who was told by the Void that the Thunderbolts did not care about him and that he did not matter. Belova denied the Void's claims, but was strangled with wires wrapping around her neck. [[File:Yelena hugs Bob.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova saves [[Bob Reynolds]] from the [[Void]]]] The team watched as Reynolds fought against the Void, but they all became concerned when Reynolds was beating the Void, as it was actually consuming him in the process. Belova yelled out for Reynolds to stop, but he continued attacking the Void. Shostakov lifted the table pinning them so that Belova could slip out, and she ran towards Reynolds and hugged him. Belova told Reynolds that he was not alone as the rest of the Thunderbolts rushed over and hugged Reynolds, stopping him from attacking the Void. [[File:Reynolds and Belova.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova promises to stay with [[Bob Reynolds]]]] The Thunderbolts were successful in freeing Reynolds from being completely consumed, which allowed them to escape the Void and return to [[New York City]], which had now been restored. Reynolds had trouble remembering what had happened, so Belova checked if he was fine, which he assured her he was now. Walker asked what they do with Reynolds if he regained his memory, so Belova grabbed Reynolds' arm and stated that from now on they all stick together, which made him happy.{{Ref|TB}} ====Member of the New Avengers==== [[File:Yelena TB ending.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova is publicly announced an [[New Avengers|Avenger]]]] {{Quote|[[New Avengers|We]]'re losing credibility."
"In which we had very little to begin with."
"And now there's this huge space crisis and no one's telling us about it.|Yelena Belova and [[Ava Starr]]|Thunderbolts*}} Spotting [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]], they team chased after her to arrest her. However, they were ambushed when de Fontaine led them to a press conference she had set up and announced the team as the [[New Avengers]]. Seeing this as an opportunity to finally be seen as a hero by the public, Belova decided to play along and whispered to de Fontaine that the team controlled her now. [[File:Yelena Belova as an Avenger.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova discusses the copyright]] As the New Avengers, the team were supplied with new costumes and transformed the [[Watchtower]] into their headquarters. Fourteen months later, the New Avengers came into conflict with [[Sam Wilson]], who was forming his own [[Avengers]], which annoyed Belova as she felt that they were the official team due to the government's approval. Belova wondered who would be viewed as the real Avengers and asked [[Bucky Barnes]] to talk with Wilson, although he already did and it went poorly. [[File:Yelena looks at F4 ship.png|{{AltFloat}}|Belova looks at a spaceship]] Belova commented on how Wilson had filed for copyright, which was making them lose credibility with the public, as well as how they were being not being informed on a space crisis that was happening. As Belova had a threat analysis ran by the Watchtower, [[Alexei Shostakov]] revealed his idea for them to rebrand as the "New Avengerz," but Belova shut him down. They received an alert, so Barnes instructed Belova to pull up a satellite image and ready the jets. The team then discovered an [[Fantastic Four's Multiversal Ship|extra-dimensional ship]] entering [[Earth]]'s orbit.{{Ref|TB}} ==Abilities== {{Quote|Yelena, you went on to become the greatest child assassin the world has ever known. No one can match your efficiency, your ruthlessness.|[[Alexei Shostakov]] to Yelena Belova|Black Widow (film)}} *'''Master Martial Artist''': [[File:Yelena Belova vs. Clint Barton.png|thumb|250px|Belova fighting against [[Hawkeye]]]] Belova is an extremely skilled and formidable hand-to-hand combatant and martial artist, having been gone through the training of the Red Room since young age in order to become a Widow, making her one of the most dangerous and best fighters on the planet. She was able to fight [[Natasha Romanoff]] evenly, matching her move for move before they had a truce. During the fight, she employed a wide variety of throws and locks. Compared to Romanoff's more acrobatic style of fighting, Belova has a more direct and hands-on way of fighting that seems heavily influenced by {{WPS|Sambo}}, including combat styles such as {{WPS|Kali}}, {{WPS|Krav Maga}}, {{WPS|Vovinam}}, {{WPS|Judo}}, {{WPS| Tae kwon do}}, {{WPS|Karate}} and {{WPS|Wrestling}}. Belova is able to quickly use her surroundings to her advantage and use random items as improvised weaponry. She was also able to easily fight off several surgeons at once while escaping the Red Room, and was able to quickly incapacitate a guard by disorientating him and sweeping his legs out from under him. Yelena was able to easily outclass [[Kate Bishop]], while simultaneously being able to keep up with, and sometimes even gain the upper hand against, the extremely skilled and experienced martial artists [[Clint Barton]] and [[Echo|Maya Lopez]], though Barton was more focused on protecting Bishop than engaging Yelena. Belova is also very physically strong and resilient, as she could flip her sister into a wall with one leg and lift Kate Bishop over her head and throw her off a building. Belova also has very good reflexes, being able to catch a bottle of hot sauce that Bishop threw at her head. At the [[O.X.E. Vault]], she was able to contend with [[U.S. Agent]] and even used a takedown on him before he retaliated with his superhuman strength. *'''Knife Mastery''': [[File:BlackWidowTeaserTrailer26.png|thumb|250px|Belova fights [[Black Widow|her sister]] with a knife]] As a Black Widow, Belova is highly skilled at using a knife in combat, being able to use it to quickly kill a [[Oksana|former Widow]] and later use it in her fight against Romanoff. Belova was also able to quickly throw a two-inch blade into a Red Room surgeon's back with great accuracy while making her escape. She fought against [[U.S. Agent]] with a knife in the [[O.X.E. Vault]] and accurately threw another at [[Ghost]], but she phased out of its path. *'''Staff Mastery''': As a Black Widow, Belova is highly skilled at using a staff in combat. *'''Master Marksman''': Belova is highly skilled and well-trained in firearms, including pistols, sniper rifles and grenade launchers. She could target the tower of the Russian gulag from a long distance. *'''Master Acrobat''': [[File:YBelova dodges counters (8).png|thumb|250px|Belova jumps flying counters]] As a Black Widow, Belova is highly skilled in incorporating gymnastics and acrobatics into her fighting style as she battles opponents. She has been very flexible since a young age, as she could already assume the upward bow position at the age of six. *'''Master Spy''': Having trained in the Red Room, Belova is highly skilled in infiltration and spycraft. She created a detailed, extensive background story for her absent family. {{Quote|You [[Natasha Romanoff|both]] have killed so many people. Your ledgers must be dripping, just gushing red.|[[Alexei Shostakov]] to Yelena Belova|Black Widow (film)}} *'''Master Assassin''': As a Black Widow, Belova is a extremely skilled assassin, being able to kill her targets with incredible efficiency. She has killed many people while she was under the control of the Red Room, including Oksana. Belova also attempted to assassinate Clint Barton, as she was instructed to by [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]]. *'''Pilot''': Belova was capable of copiloting a helicopter with [[Natasha Romanoff]], and piloting the helicopter by herself when Romanoff left to help [[Red Guardian]]. *'''Multilingualism''': Belova is fluent in her native Russian, as well as English and Hungarian. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Black Widow Batons]]''': Belova briefly utilized Black Widow Batons during the [[Destruction of the Red Room]]. Belova combined the batons into a staff and then plunged into the propeller of [[Dreykov|General Dreykov]]'s helicopter, breaking the propeller and causing the helicopter to explode, killing Dreykov and his remaining soldiers. She later used her own when fighting against Hawkeye. *'''[[Black Widow's Bite]]''': Belova utilized her Black Widow's Bite to engage Echo and Hawkeye. Belova used it to immobilize them stunning them both so she would have an advantage, as she was taking on both Lopez and Barton as well as Kate Bishop. *'''Combat Knife''': Yelena used a combat knife to kill a rouge Widow. She later used a karambit style knife to escape and kill the Red Room attendants. *'''Throwing Knives''': Yelena carries serval throwing knives on her left thigh. *'''{{WPS|Makarov pistol|Makarov PM}}''': In the [[Red Room]], Belova assembled a pistol before the other little girls could as an exercise which resulted in everyone else's hands being whipped. *'''{{WPS|Glock|Glock 26}}''': [[File:Yelena Belova (2016).png|thumb|250px|Belova aiming her pistol at her sister]] Belova's preferred weapon is a subcompact pistol. She aimed it at her sister when they reunited after 21 years, but Romanoff swiftly disarmed her. She continued to use her pistol during her campaign to liberate other Black Widows, notably bringing it to [[Ana]]'s house, and attempted to shoot [[Hawkeye]] with the pistol. *'''{{WPS|Heckler & Koch P30}}''': Belova disarmed her sister of her pistol when they reunited after 21 years. [[File:Thunderbolts* Trailer 39.png|thumb|250px|Belova with her new sidearm]] *'''{{WPS|Glock 19}}''': Belova utilized a Glock 19 during her [[Battle at the O.X.E. Vault|mission]] in the [[O.X.E. Vault]], notably firing at [[U.S. Agent]], and when their security [[Chase of Red Guardian's Limousine|chased]] the Thunderbolts. *'''{{WPS|M4 carbine|M4A1}}''': Belova used a rifle against soldiers in the [[O.X.E. Vault]]. *'''{{WPS|Accuracy International Arctic Warfare|Accuracy International Arctic Warfare Magnum}}''': Belova used a sniper rifle on an assignment in Morocco. *'''{{WPS|XM25 CDTE|Heckler & Koch XM25}}''': Belova used a grenade launcher when she and her sister rescued their father from prison. ===Other Equipment === *'''Uniform''': As a Black Widow, Belova wore a standard issue black catsuit with protective padding on her shoulders, elbows and knees, belts, two weapon holsters, and tall, heeled boots. *'''[[Yelena Belova's Vest|Vest]]''': Belova bought a vest shortly after getting released from the Red Room's mental conditioning, primarily attracted to its many pockets and making a few modifications of her own. While she was not wearing it in her reunion with Romanoff, Belova put it on after they escaped Budapest. Given she lent it to Romanoff after taking down the Red Room Academy, she would replace it with another bulletproof vest. *'''Winter Uniform''': [[Rick Mason]] provided Belova and Romanoff with white suits that would fit and blend with the snow environment of their upcoming mission in a prison. The costume had shoulder, knee, and elbow pads, as well as a holster on the back for equipment, and a belt with two thigh holsters. It became her primary costume afterwards, with Belova seen wearing it in [[Ana]]'s house two years later. *'''Tactical Uniform''': In [[2024]], Belova wore a black tactical suit, complete with full-head mask. It featured a jacket with shoulder pads, and built-in [[Black Widow's Bite|wrist tasers]]. *'''Night Vision Goggles''': Belova wore night vision goggles atop her mask while attacking Clint Barton, before he overpowered her and took out her disguise. *'''Green Uniform''': In [[2027]], Belova wore an army green tactical suit when she contacted by [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] for another mission. ===Vehicles=== *'''Motorcycle''': Belova acquired a motorcycle and used it to get to her sister, [[Natasha Romanoff]]. *'''[[Solovyov Air Helicopter]]''': Thanks to the efforts of [[Rick Mason]], Belova and Romanoff were able to use a helicopter to free the [[Red Guardian]] from prison, though the helicopter ran out of gas, and they had to abandon it. ==Facilities== *'''[[Rowley Residence]]''': Before joining the Red Room, Belova lived in Ohio with her "Family", the Rowleys in their home. She was unaware that it was just an assignment from the Red Room however. *'''[[Red Room Academy]]''': As a child, Belova trained in the Red Room Academy to eventually become a Black Widow. *'''[[Melina Vostokoff's Homestead]]''': Belova visited her mother, [[Melina Vostokoff]] at her home to find out where to find the [[Red Room]]. *'''[[Watchtower]]''': While operating as a member of the [[New Avengers]], Belova resided in and operated from the Watchtower. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Father *Mother *Adoptive Paternal Grandfather † *[[Red Guardian|Alexei Shostakov/Red Guardian]] -
